title: nama hispida subsp. spathulata
intro: Nama hispida subsp. spathulata, also known as desert heliotrope, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Nama hispida subsp. spathulata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and respiratory problems.
